title: Bridge Paper: Emotion Downregulation Targets Interoceptive Brain Regions While Emotion Upregulation Targets Other Affective Brain Regions.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Body-energy** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Interoception — visceral perception, cardiac interoception, insula, vagal signaling, felt aliveness.
**Phase context:** interoception, interoceptive, heart-brain, vagal, insula, cardiorespiratory, breathing entrainment

Paper: Emotion Downregulation Targets Interoceptive Brain Regions While Emotion Upregulation Targets Other Affective Brain Regions.
DOI: 10.1523/JNEUROSCI.1865-21.2022
Abstract: Researchers generally agree that when upregulating and downregulating emotion, control regions in the prefrontal cortex turn up or down activity in affect-generating brain areas. However, the "affective dial hypothesis" that turning up and down emotions produces opposite effects in the same affect-generating regions is untested. We tested this hypothesis by examining the overlap between the regions activated during upregulation and those deactivated during downregulation in 54 male and 51 female
